lines.translated: One mili, two mili, three mili, four.

lines.translated: One khokha, two khokha, three khokha, four.
lines.note: Transliteration: Ek khokha do khokha teen khokha chaar. Preserves slang 'khokha' for lyrical consistency.

lines.original: Oh kal si Dubai nal sheikh vi si kai
lines.translated: Yesterday in Dubai there were also many sheikhs.
lines.note: Transliteration: Oh kal si Dubai nal sheikh vi si kai. Proper nouns preserved; conservative structure used.
Transliteration: Chal.

lines.original: Ohto baad main Columbia flight fad lai
lines.translated: After that I caught a Columbia flight.
lines.note: Transliteration: Ohto baad main Columbia flight fad lai. Kept 'Columbia' as in original.

lines.original: Jithe java othe yaar hi kamaaye jatt ne
lines.translated: Wherever I go, the jatt earns with his friends.
lines.note: Transliteration: Jithe java othe yaar hi kamaaye jatt ne. 'jatt' preserved per glossary guidance.

lines.original: Tainu lagda ke paise di kamayi kar lai
lines.translated: You think you should go earn some money.
lines.note: Transliteration: Tainu lagda ke paise di kamayi kar lai.
Transliteration: Chaal jatt di slow. Implies deliberate, proud swagger.
Transliteration: Mainu dekho gaur naal.

lines.original: Kalla kalla putt ture torra'n taur naal
lines.translated: Every lone son walks with his own swagger
lines.note: Transliteration: Kalla kalla putt ture torra'n taur naal. Conservative rendering keeps the idea of individual swagger.
Transliteration: Yeah they know.
Transliteration: Meri kithe tak maar.

lines.original: Akhan vicho parh lva banda mile saar
lines.translated: I can read you by your eyes; the man earns respect
lines.note: Transliteration: Akhan vicho parh lva banda mile saar. 'saar' conservatively rendered as 'respect'.

lines.original: Mangey jhanjara di jodi ehio masla
lines.translated: Even asking for a pair of anklet bells causes this problem
lines.note: Transliteration: Mangey jhanjara di jodi ehio masla. 'jhanjara' = anklet bells (ghungroo).

lines.translated: The mare asks for a 'badama-ala' platter
lines.note: Transliteration: Ghodi mangdi badama ala tasla. 'badama-ala' left untranslated due to uncertainty.

lines.original: Baahla manga na swaad na swaadi aa
lines.translated: I didn't ask for elegance — neither taste nor a tasteful one
lines.note: Transliteration: Bahla ruka na rekaane kite maaran ikk fera. Conservative rendering following original ambiguity.

lines.original: Asi rotia achaara'n de hi aaadi aa
lines.translated: We're used to rotis and pickles
lines.note: Transliteration: Asi rotia achaara'n de hi aaadi aa. Conveys simple roots and food imagery.

lines.original: Jithe java naal vdda bai har vaar
lines.translated: Wherever I go, my big brother is always with me
lines.note: 'vdda bai' = elder/big brother; literal phrasing preserved.

lines.translated: Why show it off out in the open now?
lines.note: 'fukna' literally 'to blow' — in slang can mean 'to flaunt/flash' money; chose conservative 'show off'.
Phrase is brief and ambiguous — could reference a model/position/timing; preserved literal order.

lines.translated: We keep our gear all black
lines.note: 'gunjee' is uncertain—interpreted as gear/kit/clothes in this context; chose a natural idiomatic rendering.
‘Ration’ used in South Asian sense of staple supplies; chose conservative household phrasing.
Spelling 'slaaping' likely intends 'slapping' (slang for 'great/hard-hitting'); rendered as common idiom.
May be idiomatic: 'extra daant' can imply someone is overly aggressive, cheeky, or has an extra 'bite'. Kept literal and noted.
“Cappin'” kept as slang — generally means showing off or lying.

Transliteration: 'Na kiya shadi shows'. Straightforward statement about not performing at weddings.

lines.translated: There's a deep drain beneath you
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Gehra nala tere niche'. Preserved the image without adding interpretation.
